subject: Sociology
title: News as Social Construction
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on News as Social Construction.

hook.text: Thousands of events happen daily. Only a dozen make the news. Who decides what matters?

buildup.text: News isn't a mirror of events. Editors, journalists, and algorithms select, frame, and package reality.

discovery.text: News values determine coverage: conflict, novelty, celebrity, and proximity get attention. Slow crises get ignored.

twist.text: One missing white woman gets months of coverage. Thousands of missing minorities get silence. Selection is political.

climax.text: News doesn't report reality. It constructs a version of reality shaped by commercial and ideological interests.

punchline.text: News is a story about what matters — and to whom.

quiz.question: What does it mean that news is 'socially constructed'?
options: Events are selected, framed, and packaged by human choices
